summary: Implemented a tabbed React UI with three calculators (Pressure, Temperature, Flow), calculation utilities with unit conversions, and validation helpers plus an industrial-themed Tailwind styling. However, Internet Identity/auth + actor hooks were added despite being a stated non-goal, and Kelvin validation appears incomplete for negative Kelvin inputs when Kelvin is the input unit (not the output).

missing_requirements.requirement: Temperature converter prevents invalid Kelvin (< 0 K).
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows Kelvin validation only applied when converting *to* Kelvin (toUnit === 'K'). If the user selects Kelvin as the *from* unit, negative Kelvin inputs are not shown as prevented/validated in the provided code, which would allow invalid Kelvin values.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication context/provider and actor creation hooks using React Query.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest explicitly lists Internet Identity authentication as a non-goal, but the diff adds an InternetIdentityProvider, useInternetIdentity hook, and useActor hook integrating identity and actor creation.

hallucinated_features.description: Footer attribution link to caffeine.ai ('Built with love...')
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by any requirement; adds extra user-facing content/branding beyond the calculator tool UI.
No backend functionality was requested (and persisting calculations/history was a non-goal); an empty backend actor was introduced anyway.
